test.name: Connect historical context to an active research project
test.description: Tests whether the skill bridges general history to the specific research at hand. Different from the stateless tests where context stands alone — here the context should inform the existing assertions and questions in the scenario.

input.user_message: Given what I'm researching about Patrick Flynn — born Ireland ~1845, in Schuylkill County by 1850 — what historical context from his lifetime should inform what records I should be looking for?

additional_criteria: Should ground the context in Patrick's actual timeline (Irish-born ~1845, in PA by 1850, died 1908) — not produce generic 19th-century context

Should suggest specific record classes that follow from the context — e.g., Castle Garden / Ellis Island immigration records (immigration triggered by famine), naturalization records (likely filed in adulthood), mine-worker rosters
Should respect the boundary: this skill produces historical context, not search plans. Mentioning record classes is appropriate; producing a formal `plans[]` entry is not (that's research-plan)
